records.ety: [Icel. skraema to scare, terrify; akin to Sw. skräma, Dan. skraemme. Cf. Screech.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Screamed (skrēmd); p. pr. & vb. n. Screaming.]

defs: To cry out with a shrill voice; to utter a sudden, sharp outcry, or shrill, loud cry, as in fright or extreme pain; to shriek; to screech.
quotes: I heard the owl scream and the crickets cry.
And scream thyself as none e'er screamed before.

defs: A sharp, shrill cry, uttered suddenly, as in terror or in pain; a shriek; a screech.
